q: The fluid statics concentrates on the fluids in motion.
The surface tension is a concept of hydrodynamics.
The capillarity is a concept of hydrodynamics.
Fluid is either liquid or gas.
Fluid is liquid only.
Fluid is gas only.
The volume of a gas depends on the pressure.
The volume of a gas depends on the temperature.
The volume of a liquid varies with pressure at a constant temperature.
The liquid is compressible.
The gas is incompressible.
The density of the liquid is constant for any pressure at a constant temperature.
If the flow or the path of the fluid particles is irregular, it is a laminar flow.
If the flow of the fluid particles are always changing their directions, it is laminar flow.
If the flow or the path of the fluid particles is always whirling, it is turbulent flow.
In laminar flow, the velocity of the fluid at a point is continuously changing.
If the fluid particles are moving steadily in smooth paths in layers, it is a turbulent flow.
In a turbulent flow, each layer of the fluid moves smoothly past the adjacent layers separately.
In turbulent flow, the streamlines do not cross each other..
For ideal laminar fluid flow, the fluid is incompressible.
For ideal laminar fluid flow, the fluid flow has friction.
For ideal laminar fluid flow, the friction may be neglected.
For ideal fluid flow, the fluid flow is laminar.
For ideal fluid flow, the fluid flow is turbulent.
The sea breeze is a laminar flow.
The storm is a laminar flow.
Motor car brake oil is incompressible.
The study of the flow of liquid is called hydrostatics.
Gases are compressible.
Fluid is a substance that offers permanent resistance to deforming forces.
The fluid dynamics concerns the fluid in motion.
The study of liquid at rest is called hydrodynamics.
In an ideal laminar flow tube, the mass flow rate is constant.
In an ideal laminar flow tube, the volume flow rate is constant.
In an ideal laminar flow tube, the product of the flow speed and the cross-sectional area is always constant.
In an ideal laminar flow tube, the flow speed is always constant.
The liquid entering one end of the pipe leaves from the other end with a constant volume flow rate.
The larger the area of the pipe, the slower the speed of the fluid.
The smaller the area of the pipe, the faster the speed of the fluid.
In the flow tube that obeys Bernoulli's equation, the internal friction of the fluid flow is negligible.
The work done is equal to the change in total mechanical energy.
Mechanical energy is the sum of potential energy and kinetic energy.
The sum of the pressure energy, the kinetic energy and the potential energy per unit volume of an incompressible, non-viscous fluid in a streamlined laminar flow remains constant.
The kinetic energy of an incompressible, non-viscous fluid in a streamlined laminar flow remains constant.
The potential energy per unit volume of an incompressible, non-viscous fluid in a streamlined laminar flow remains constant.
The pressure energy per unit volume of an incompressible, non-viscous fluid in a streamlined laminar flow remains constant.
For a flying plane, the air flow speed is higher above the wing than below the wing.
For a flying plane, the air pressure is higher above the wing than below the wing.
The air flow velocity is high along the top surface of a flying plane.
The velocity of the airflow is low along the bottom surface of a flying plane.
When the piston of the sprayer is pressed, the fast moving air causes a high pressure at the tip of the nozzle.
When the piston of the sprayer is pressed, the liquid inside the container is at a lower pressure.
The dragging of air by the spinning ball causes the difference in air pressure on the opposite sides of the spinning ball.
During cyclones, moving air gains speed above the roof of a house.
During cyclones, the pressure on the roof is higher than the pressure inside the house.
Most of the fluids are ideal ones.
Most of the fluids offer some resistance to motion.
Viscosity can be thought of as a measure of a fluid's thickness.
Viscosity can be thought of as a measure of a fluid's stickiness.
Viscosity can be thought of as a measure of a fluid's resistance to objects passing through it.
A fluid with a large viscosity resists motion.
A fluid with low viscosity flows easily.
Gases do not exhibit viscosity.
It is harder to notice the viscosity of gases in ordinary circumstances.
The viscosity of liquids increases rapidly with an increase in temperature.
The viscosity of gases increases with a decrease in temperature.
Upon heating, liquids flow more easily.
Upon heating, gases flow more quickly.
Viscosity does not change as the amount of matter changes.
Viscosity is an extensive property.
The shear stress is directly proportional to the velocity gradient.
The shear stress is the normal force per unit area.
Surface tension is due to the attractive forces called adhesion only.
Cohesion refers to forces between molecules of different substances.
Adhesion refers to forces between molecules of the same substance.
Paperclips can rest on the water without sinking if there is enough surface tension.
The density of a paperclip is greater than that of water.
Razor blades can rest on the water without sinking if there is enough surface tension.
The density of a razor blade is greater than that of water.
Light coins can rest on the water without sinking if there is enough surface tension.
The density of a light coin is greater than that of water.
The surface tension is any force per unit length of any object.
Adhesion refers to forces between molecules of the same substance.
Cohesion refers to forces between molecules of different substances.
A capillary tube needs to be sufficiently large in diameter for wicking to occur.
Capillary action is the rise or depression of a liquid in a narrow tube.
Capillary action can occur between the fibres of a towel.
Capillary action can occur between the openings in a porous material.
The rise of water underneath the ground is an example of capillary action.
When a glass tube with a small inside diameter is dipped into water, the water rises into the tube.
The smaller the bore of the tube, the lower the capillary rise.
Liquids that are depressed within thin tubes below the surface of the surrounding liquid do not wet the tube.
Water wets the glass capillary tubes.
Mercury wets the glass capillary tubes.
Capillarity does not occur for non-wetting liquids.
For a wetting liquid, the adhesive force is greater than the cohesive force.
For a non-wetting liquid, the adhesive force is greater than the cohesive force.
